list.text: 3(9x)+2(5y)=27   ‚àí3(9x)+2(5y)=9 The solution to the given system of ‚Å£‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äç‚Äç‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Äç‚Äç‚Äå‚Äå‚Å§equations is  (x,y). What is the value of 9x+5y? (round to nearest integer if necessary)

first the values of x and y must be found. this can be done by rearranging the equations to be 3(9x)+2(5y)-27=0 and -3(9x)+2(5y)-9=0. Then the equations can be set equal to each other. 3(9x)+2(5y)-27=-3(9x)+2(5y)-9. This can be done on desmos by graphing the equations 3(9x)+2(5y)=27 and ‚àí3(9x)+2(5y)=9 and checking the point where they intersect. This is equivalent to setting the two equations equal to each other, as the x and y values of both graphs have to be equal at the point of intersection for them to intersect. The point of intersection is (0.33333,1.8), so x=0.33333 and y=1.8. to find the value of 9x+5y, the values of x and y can be plugged in to get 9(0.33333)+5(1.8)=12. therefore, the answer is 12.
